commit.message:
Comprehensive security hardening for production readiness

- CRITICAL: Gate x-user-id header fallback behind NODE_ENV (was auth bypass in production)
- CRITICAL: Fix user invite to derive userId from JWT instead of trusting req.body
- HIGH: Fix Stripe checkout/portal to authenticate via JWT instead of body-supplied userId
- HIGH: Add authentication to email, ingredient/recipe, file upload/serve endpoints
- HIGH: Add JWT auth + role checks to kiosk update-pin and set-code endpoints
- HIGH: Add tenant ownership checks to subscription, billing-details, referral endpoints
- HIGH: Harden dev mode check (require import.meta.env.DEV in addition to VITE_USE_MOCK_DATA)
- MEDIUM: Add helmet for security headers (X-Frame-Options, HSTS, etc.)
- MEDIUM: Add CORS middleware with explicit configuration
- MEDIUM: Add escapeHtml utility to prevent XSS in email templates and PDF exports
- MEDIUM: Add Square OAuth CSRF state validation with one-time-use tokens
- MEDIUM: Add file type allowlist and 50MB size limit on upload endpoint
- MEDIUM: Genericize all 50+ error.message leaks to 'Internal server error'
- MEDIUM: Remove debug info from 403 platform admin response
- MEDIUM: Fix DB SSL to use rejectUnauthorized:true in production
- MEDIUM: Add JSON body size limit (1MB) and truncate response body logging
- LOW: Remove unused dependencies (passport, express-session, memorystore, connect-pg-simple)
- Update client calls to send Authorization Bearer headers for all protected endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(fetchpet): bypass MuiDialog scroll interception on submit button click (0.1.3) (#392)

## Summary

Fix `submit_claim` failing with a 30-second timeout because the
MuiDialog scroll container intercepts pointer events on the submit
button (#391).

**Root cause:** The claim form is rendered as a MuiDialog
(`MuiDialog-root generic-dialog`) whose content is taller than the
viewport (submit button at Y≈1308 vs viewport height 1080). The
`MuiDialog-container MuiDialog-scrollPaper` div intercepts pointer
events, causing Playwright's `click()` to time out.

**Fix:** Replace `submitButton.click()` with `page.evaluate()` that
calls `scrollIntoView({ block: 'center' })` + `click()` in JavaScript,
bypassing Playwright's actionability checks. This is implemented as a
new `clickButtonInDialog()` helper method.

**What was removed:** The initial (wrong) approach added a generic
`dismissMuiDialog()` method that tried to dismiss a separate overlay.
Investigation against the real portal revealed there IS no separate
overlay — the claim form itself is the MuiDialog causing the
interception.

## Verification

- [x] E2E: Reproduced the bug against the real Fetch Pet portal —
`prepare_claim_to_submit` succeeded (Nova, Twin Cities Vet Hospital,
$53.52, Nexgard Plus), then `submit_claim` failed with
`elementHandle.click: Timeout 30000ms exceeded` — `MuiDialog-container
MuiDialog-scrollPaper` intercepts pointer events
- [x] E2E: Used Playwright debug scripts against the real portal to
investigate root cause — confirmed only 1 MuiDialog exists (the claim
form itself, NOT a separate overlay), submit button at Y=1308 vs
viewport 1080, `scrollIntoViewIfNeeded()` + `click()` still fails, but
`page.evaluate()` with `scrollIntoView()` + `click()` succeeds
- [x] Functional tests: 7/7 PASS (all existing tests pass)
- [x] Build succeeds with no lint errors
- [x] Self-reviewed PR diff — no unintended changes, temporary debug
scripts removed

---------

Co-authored-by: Agent Orchestrator <agent-orchestrator@pulsemcp.com>

actually see, hear, feel, or smell:

- "fascia" → "gutters are straight and drain properly"
- "grading" → "ground slopes away so water doesn't pool"
- "single-pane" → "feel outside air coming through them"
- "builder-grade" → "original cabinets and countertops"
- "galvanized pipes" → "very low water pressure from old pipes"
- "knob-and-tube" → "old wiring in the attic or basement"
- "structural piers" → "foundation is shifting, walls are bowing"
- "broken seals" → "windows fog up between the glass"

Written so a 6th grader could read it and immediately know which
tier their home falls into.

commit.message:
Add 'On Structure' post (Mar 19): structural realism + SAE universality

Three research programs — NCC, LLM interpretability, protein LM
interpretability — all try to bridge levels of description using structure
as the middle term. The protein LM case (Adams et al. ICML 2025,
InterPLM Nature Methods 2025) provides the strongest current evidence
for structural realism: convergence across independent models, validated
against external biological reality. Addresses seed stability problem
(30% feature overlap) and Newman objection head-on.

commit.message:
Fix session auth: store Bearer token to survive cross-origin cookie failures

The backend already returns session_token in the POST /auth/google response
body AND already reads Authorization: Bearer as a fallback in
get_session_from_request. The token was never being stored or sent.

Changes (frontend only — backend needs no changes):

1. Global axios interceptor (module-level, runs before every request):
   - Reads chintan_session_token from sessionStorage
   - Injects Authorization: Bearer <token> header if present and not
     already set by the caller
   - Works alongside cookies; whichever reaches the server first wins

2. axios.defaults.withCredentials = true — applies globally so individual
   call-site { withCredentials: true } options become redundant but harmless

3. login(userData, token = null) — AuthProvider now accepts and stores the
   token from /auth/google response into sessionStorage

4. AuthCallback passes response.data.session_token to login() so the
   interceptor has a token available for the immediate next request
   (e.g. PUT /users/interests) before the browser has stored the cookie

5. logout() clears chintan_session_token from sessionStorage

sessionStorage was chosen over localStorage: persists across in-tab
navigation (including React Router), cleared when tab closes, not
accessible by other tabs (no cross-tab leakage).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(crm): ленивая загрузка вложений из IMAP — скачивать только по запросу

Вложения при синхронизации не скачиваются на диск, сохраняются только
метаданные (filename, size, imap_uid, imap_folder). Файл скачивается из
IMAP только когда пользователь нажимает кнопку скачивания во фронтенде.

Это устраняет EACCES-ошибки и Socket timeout при синхронизации почты.

commit.message:
fix(daily): init serverDate with local date to prevent stuck loading screen

serverDate started as null, causing the loading guard (!serverDate || !loaded)
to block the page until Firebase .info/serverTimeOffset responded. On slow
mobile connections this caused a persistent loading screen. Now initializes
with local date immediately; Firebase refines it if there's timezone drift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add TruncateFilter to cap reolink-aio debug log message length

Large JSON/XML payloads from reolink-aio debug logs are now truncated
to 500 chars by default. The filter is attached to the reolink_aio
logger for NVR_DEBUG=full or the reolink_aio.api logger for
NVR_DEBUG=true, keeping log output readable while preserving the
original message length in a suffix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): remove Ajax Spider from ZAP; fix artifact upload

Remove -a (Ajax Spider) from ZAP cmd_options:
- Ajax Spider launches a headless Firefox browser which makes requests to
  firefox-settings-attachments.cdn.mozilla.net, generating 7 external alerts
  that triggered a false GitHub Issue. REST APIs don't need JS rendering.

Remove artifact_name from ZAP action:
- Not a valid parameter in action-full-scan@v0.10.0; caused internal artifact
  upload to fail with HTTP 400. The separate upload-artifact step handles it.

Rename artifact to zap-dast-report and include both HTML and JSON reports.

commit.message:
fix: prevent compose job toctou race and catch handler rejections (#3746)

* fix: prevent compose job toctou race and catch handler rejections

Add partial unique index on compose_jobs to enforce one active job per
user at the DB level. Use INSERT...ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING to atomically
check-and-insert. Wrap catch handler db.update() calls in try/catch to
prevent unhandled rejections when the database is unavailable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace non-null assertions with explicit null check in compose job conflict path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use advisory lock to prevent run concurrency limit race (#3745)

* fix: use advisory lock to prevent run concurrency limit race condition

Wrap the concurrency check and INSERT in a database transaction with
pg_advisory_xact_lock to serialize per-user, eliminating the TOCTOU
window where concurrent requests could bypass the limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update jsdoc pipeline to reflect advisory lock transaction

* fix: align inline step numbers with jsdoc and add concurrent race test

Renumber inline comments (Steps 1-9) to match JSDoc pipeline numbering.
Add regression test that fires two concurrent createRun() calls to
verify the advisory lock correctly serializes per-user run creation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle invalid json in github oauth callback state parameter (#3744)

* fix: handle invalid json in github oauth callback state parameter

Wrap JSON.parse(state) in try/catch to prevent uncaught exceptions
when the state parameter contains invalid JSON, matching the pattern
used in the Slack OAuth callback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle invalid json in github oauth callback at caller level

Move JSON parse error handling from parseOAuthState to the GET handler,
returning an explicit error redirect instead of silently falling back to
null state values. This follows fail-fast principles and prevents HMAC
signature verification bypass on tampered state parameters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(mslang): move GC remove_white() to correct position in collect_garbage()

strings_.remove_white() was called before collect_garbage() in
VM::allocate(), where all objects have is_marked=false from the
previous sweep, causing all interned strings to be incorrectly
removed. Moved it into collect_garbage() after trace_references()
but before sweep(), matching the clox reference implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add autofix to 51 lint rules (phases 1-3)

Wire up Fix emission for 51 rules that already declared FixKind but
used report_warning/report_error (which sets fix: None). Each rule now
emits Diagnostic with Fix { edits } containing correct spans and
replacement text.

Phase 1 (8 rules): keyword/token replacement — prefer_const, text_encoding,
unicode_bom, prefer_code_point, prefer_string_slice, no_useless_return,
no_unused_labels, no_extra_label

Phase 2 (10 rules): deletion/insertion — no_irregular_whitespace,
no_useless_escape, no_extra_boolean_cast, no_extra_bind, no_useless_computed_key,
no_hex_escape, missing_throw, radix, require_array_join_separator,
ts/no_useless_empty_export

Phase 3A (10 rules): operator/expression swaps — bad_bitwise_operator,
double_comparisons, misrefactored_assign_op, operator_assignment, yoda,
no_eq_null, no_null, no_object_constructor, no_array_constructor, no_new_array

Phase 3B (8 rules): insertion/substitution — radix, require_array_join_separator,
prefer_date_now, prefer_exponentiation_operator, prefer_math_trunc,
no_implicit_coercion, numeric_separators_style, prefer_string_raw

Phase 3C (7 rules): medium-complexity — no_regex_spaces, prefer_numeric_literals,
prefer_object_has_own, no_div_regex, prefer_object_spread, prefer_reflect_apply,
no_new_buffer

Phase 3D (6 rules): high-complexity — no_else_return,
prefer_native_coercion_functions, prefer_prototype_methods,
prefer_string_starts_ends_with, prefer_template, prefer_ternary

74 fix idempotency tests, 3213 unit tests pass, clippy clean.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>
